notes: TX uses a 4-level hierarchy: Domain (Roman numeral) → Strand (letter) → Sub-Strand (numeric) → Indicator. Indicators come in side-by-side PK3/PK4 columns — each column is a SEPARATE indicator with its own code (e.g. PK3.I.A.2 vs PK4.I.A.2).

doc_depths.example: PK3.I.A.2 Child can identify own physical attributes ...

elements.title: Child can identify own physical attributes and indicate some likes and dislikes when prompted.
Child shows self-awareness of physical attributes, personal preferences, and own abilities.

regression_cases.description: PK3 and PK4 outcomes for the same skill row must each emit their own indicator.
regression_cases.assertion: for every skill row, the detected output contains at least one indicator with code starting 'PK3.' AND at least one with code starting 'PK4.'.

regression_cases.description: Age band must be populated for every indicator.
The literal text 'PK3 Outcome' / 'PK4 Outcome' column headers must not be extracted as indicators.
regression_cases.assertion: no indicator has title in {'PK3 Outcome', 'PK4 Outcome', 'PK3', 'PK4'}.
